name: Espirito Santo (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the rosa-velho colour from Princess Leopoldina's mantle

description: Three horizontal stripes, blue at the top, a narrower white in the middle and rose-pink at the bottom, with the inscription TRABALHA E CONFIA in blue letters on the central white band.
history: Designed by Antonio Aguirre and Eulalia de Aguirre and adopted by State Decree No. 5 on 24 May 1908; the colours are taken from the mantle of Princess Leopoldina, with the motto from Ignatius of Loyola.

features.symbolism: Three horizontal bands with the state motto on the central white stripe; the colours are taken from the cope worn by Princess Leopoldina
TRABALHA E CONFIA ('Work and trust'), the motto of Espirito Santo, taken from a phrase by the Jesuit Saint Ignatius of Loyola
